description: Canonical-form expansion of arrays-of-records emitted in the nested record-block form with relative `{.subarr[] : ~}` primitive-array sub-blocks. The canonical form must flatten every record's nested data into explicit absolute paths regardless of the input shape.

tests.description: Ragged string sub-arrays expand to fully-qualified absolute paths
tests.expected:
records[0].name = "Alice"
records[0].tags[0] = "red"
records[0].tags[1] = "green"
records[0].tags[2] = "blue"
records[1].name = "Bob"
records[1].tags[0] = "yellow"

tests.description: Records with multiple distinct primitive-array sub-blocks expand cleanly
tests.expected:
entries[0].fields[0] = "id"
entries[0].fields[1] = "name"
entries[0].slug = "a/one"
entries[0].types[0] = "alpha"
entries[0].types[1] = "beta"
entries[1].fields[0] = "id"
entries[1].slug = "b/two"
entries[1].types[0] = "gamma"

tests.description: Tabular form with key[N] columns canonicalizes to flat indexed paths
tests.expected:
points[0].coords[0] = ##1
points[0].coords[1] = ##2
points[0].label = "A"
points[1].coords[0] = ##3
points[1].coords[1] = ##4
points[1].label = "B"
